evidence.detail: R1 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1/30) <-> R2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.2/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R1 neighbor 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.2 (ospf-nbr-full), R2 neighbor 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.1 (ospf-nbr-full).
R1 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.10/30) <-> R3 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.9/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R1 neighbor 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.9, R3 neighbor 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.10 (both ospf-nbr-full).
R2 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.5/30) <-> R3 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.6/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R2 neighbor 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.6, R3 neighbor 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.5 (both ospf-nbr-full).

recent_changes.summary: Discovered routers R1, R2, R3 with running-config and OSPF/DHCP details.
Added link R1:Serial0/1/0 <-> R2:Serial0/1/0 (observed via OSPF neighbors).
Added link R1:Serial0/1/1 <-> R3:Serial0/1/0 (observed via OSPF neighbors).
Added link R2:Serial0/1/1 <-> R3:Serial0/1/1 (observed via OSPF neighbors).

facts.claim: R1 interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 192.168.0.1/24 (desc LAN); Serial0/1/0 10.0.0.1/30 (desc Link to R2); Serial0/1/1 10.0.0.10/30 (desc Link to R3).
R2 interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 172.16.0.1/24 (desc LAN); Serial0/1/0 10.0.0.2/30 (desc Link to R1, proxy-arp false); Serial0/1/1 10.0.0.5/30 (desc Link to R3).
R3 interfaces: Loopback1 203.0.113.1/32 (desc Simulated Internet); Serial0/1/0 10.0.0.9/30 (desc Link to R1); Serial0/1/1 10.0.0.6/30 (desc Link to R2).
OSPF: R1 process 1 router-id 16843009 (Serial0/1/0 neigh 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.2 cost49; Serial0/1/1 neigh 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.9 cost49; GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost1).
OSPF: R2 process 1 router-id 33686018 (Serial0/1/0 neigh 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.1 cost50; Serial0/1/1 neigh 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.6 cost50; GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost1).
OSPF: R3 process 1 router-id 50529027 (Loopback1 loopback; Serial0/1/1 neigh 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.5 cost49; Serial0/1/0 neigh 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.10 cost50).
DHCP: R1 pool R1_LAN network 192.168.0.0/24 default-router 192.168.0.1 dns 8.8.8.8 domain lab-local excluded 192.168.0.1-192.168.0.10.
DHCP: R2 pool R2_LAN network 172.16.0.0/24 default-router 172.16.0.1 dns 8.8.8.8 excluded 172.16.0.1-172.16.0.254.

root_causes.cause: Server 172.16.0.12 likely has no valid DHCP address (or no address) because R2's DHCP configuration explicitly excludes the entire 172.16.0.0/24 pool.
evidence: get_dhcp_config (R2): "Cisco-IOS-XE-dhcp:excluded-address": { "low-high-address-list": [{ "low-address": "172.16.0.1", "high-address": "172.16.0.254" }] } (excludes whole /24)
get_running_config (R2): GigabitEthernet0/0/0 configured 172.16.0.1/24 (shows 172.16.0.0/24 is the LAN where 172.16.0.12 would reside)
alert_description: 25 ICMP attempts to 172.16.0.12, 0 replies (no responses observed)

missing_info: Is 172.16.0.12 statically configured on the server (or otherwise assigned) or expected to obtain its address via DHCP?
Is the server powered on and physically connected to R2's LAN (any link/port status or switchport show)?
Does R2 have an ARP entry for 172.16.0.12 (show ip arp 172.16.0.12)?
From which device/IP were the 25 ICMP attempts sourced (to confirm path and source reachability)?
Are there host-based firewall rules on the server blocking ICMP?

data.intent_description: Host 172.16.0.12 is unreachable (25 ICMP attempts, 0 replies). R2's DHCP configuration currently excludes the entire 172.16.0.0/24, likely preventing the server from obtaining an IP. Goal: restore reachability by allowing 172.16.0.12 to obtain an address (remove exclusion) or create a static binding.

plan.problem: R2 DHCP excluded-address list contains low=172.16.0.1 high=172.16.0.254 (full /24 excluded), preventing DHCP assignment in 172.16.0.0/24; server 172.16.0.12 has no responses to ICMP.
plan.fix_summary: On router2 remove the DHCP excluded-address range 172.16.0.1–172.16.0.254 so the DHCP pool for 172.16.0.0/24 can assign addresses. Assumption: 172.16.0.12 is expected to use DHCP. Operational risk: devices previously excluded may receive addresses; schedule window if needed.

parsed.intent_description: Host 172.16.0.12 is unreachable (25 ICMP attempts, 0 replies). Is the server powered on and physically connected to R2's LAN (any link/port status or switchport show)?
Does R2 have an ARP entry for 172.16.0.12 (show ip arp 172.16.0.12)?
From which device/IP were the 25 ICMP attempts sourced (to confirm path and source reachability)?
Are there host-based firewall rules on the server blocking ICMP?

plan.problem: R2 DHCP excluded-address list contains low=172.16.0.1 high=172.16.0.254 (full /24 excluded), preventing DHCP assignment in 172.16.0.0/24; server 172.16.0.12 has no responses to ICMP.
plan.fix_summary: On router2 remove the DHCP excluded-address range 172.16.0.1–172.16.0.254 so the DHCP pool for 172.16.0.0/24 can assign addresses. Assumption: 172.16.0.12 is expected to use DHCP. Operational risk: devices previously excluded may receive addresses; schedule window if needed.

evidence.detail: R1 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1/30) <-> R2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.2/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R1 neighbor 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.2 (ospf-nbr-full), R2 neighbor 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.1 (ospf-nbr-full).
R1 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.10/30) <-> R3 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.9/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R1 neighbor 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.9, R3 neighbor 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.10 (both ospf-nbr-full).
R2 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.5/30) <-> R3 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.6/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R2 neighbor 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.6, R3 neighbor 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.5 (both ospf-nbr-full).

recent_changes.summary: Discovered routers R1, R2, R3 with running-config and OSPF/DHCP details.
Added link R1:Serial0/1/0 <-> R2:Serial0/1/0 (observed via OSPF neighbors).
Added link R1:Serial0/1/1 <-> R3:Serial0/1/0 (observed via OSPF neighbors).
Added link R2:Serial0/1/1 <-> R3:Serial0/1/1 (observed via OSPF neighbors).

facts.claim: R1 interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 192.168.0.1/24 (desc LAN); Serial0/1/0 10.0.0.1/30 (desc Link to R2); Serial0/1/1 10.0.0.10/30 (desc Link to R3).
R2 interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 172.16.0.1/24 (desc LAN); Serial0/1/0 10.0.0.2/30 (desc Link to R1, proxy-arp false); Serial0/1/1 10.0.0.5/30 (desc Link to R3).
R3 interfaces: Loopback1 203.0.113.1/32 (desc Simulated Internet); Serial0/1/0 10.0.0.9/30 (desc Link to R1); Serial0/1/1 10.0.0.6/30 (desc Link to R2).
OSPF: R1 process 1 router-id 16843009 (Serial0/1/0 neigh 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.2 cost49; Serial0/1/1 neigh 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.9 cost49; GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost1).
OSPF: R2 process 1 router-id 33686018 (Serial0/1/0 neigh 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.1 cost50; Serial0/1/1 neigh 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.6 cost50; GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost1).
OSPF: R3 process 1 router-id 50529027 (Loopback1 loopback; Serial0/1/1 neigh 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.5 cost49; Serial0/1/0 neigh 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.10 cost50).
DHCP: R1 pool R1_LAN network 192.168.0.0/24 default-router 192.168.0.1 dns 8.8.8.8 domain lab-local excluded 192.168.0.1-192.168.0.10.
DHCP: R2 pool R2_LAN network 172.16.0.0/24 default-router 172.16.0.1 dns 8.8.8.8 excluded 172.16.0.1-172.16.0.254.

evidence: Most recent verification (get_dhcp_config on router2) shows the R2 DHCP pool R2_LAN for network 172.16.0.0/24 with default-router 172.16.0.1, dns-server 8.8.8.8 and domain-name lab-local, and the returned JSON does not include any Cisco-IOS-XE-dhcp:excluded-address element for that pool.

Tool output (excerpt): {"Cisco-IOS-XE-native:dhcp": {"Cisco-IOS-XE-dhcp:pool": [{"id": "R2_LAN","default-router": {"default-router-list": ["172.16.0.1"]},"dns-server": {"dns-server-list": ["8.8.8.8"]},"domain-name": "lab-local","network": {"primary-network": {"number": "172.16.0.0","mask": "255.255.255.0"}}}]}}
remaining_issues: Confirm whether 172.16.0.12 obtained a DHCP lease (or is statically configured) — check the server IP and R2 DHCP bindings (show ip dhcp binding).
Verify reachability to 172.16.0.12 (ping from R2 or another LAN host) and check R2 ARP table for 172.16.0.12 (show ip arp 172.16.0.12).
Verify the server is powered on and physically connected to the R2 LAN (check switchport/link status).
Check host-based firewall settings on 172.16.0.12 that may block ICMP.

data.intent_description: Host 172.16.0.12 is unreachable (25 ICMP attempts, 0 replies). R2's DHCP configuration currently excludes the entire 172.16.0.0/24, likely preventing the server from obtaining an IP. Goal: restore reachability by allowing 172.16.0.12 to obtain an address (remove exclusion) or create a static binding.

evidence.detail: R1 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.1/30) <-> R2 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.2/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R1 neighbor 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.2 (ospf-nbr-full), R2 neighbor 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.1 (ospf-nbr-full).
R1 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.10/30) <-> R3 Serial0/1/0 (10.0.0.9/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R1 neighbor 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.9, R3 neighbor 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.10 (both ospf-nbr-full).
R2 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.5/30) <-> R3 Serial0/1/1 (10.0.0.6/30). Seen as OSPF neighbors: R2 neighbor 3.3.3.3@10.0.0.6, R3 neighbor 2.2.2.2@10.0.0.5 (both ospf-nbr-full).

root_causes.cause: Server 172.16.0.12 likely has no valid DHCP address (or no address) because R2's DHCP configuration explicitly excludes the entire 172.16.0.0/24 pool.
evidence: get_dhcp_config (R2): "Cisco-IOS-XE-dhcp:excluded-address": { "low-high-address-list": [{ "low-address": "172.16.0.1", "high-address": "172.16.0.254" }] } (excludes whole /24)
get_running_config (R2): GigabitEthernet0/0/0 configured 172.16.0.1/24 (shows 172.16.0.0/24 is the LAN where 172.16.0.12 would reside)

messages.content:
What was observed
- 25 ICMP attempts to 172.16.0.12 with 0 replies.
- R2's DHCP pool for 172.16.0.0/24 was configured with an excluded-address range covering 172.16.0.1–172.16.0.254 (i.e. entire /24).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- 172.16.0.12 likely had no DHCP address because R2 was excluding the entire LAN from leasing.

Changes made
- On router2 I removed the DHCP excluded-address range 172.16.0.1–172.16.0.254 (delete excluded-address).

Verification
- Passed: get_dhcp_config on R2 now shows the R2_LAN pool for 172.16.0.0/24 without any excluded-address entry.
- Remaining checks (still required):
  - Confirm 172.16.0.12 obtained a lease (show ip dhcp binding or check the server IP).
  - Verify reachability (ping/traceroute from R2 or a LAN host) and check R2 ARP for 172.16.0.12 (show ip arp 172.16.0.12).
  - Confirm server is powered/connected and that host firewall isn’t blocking ICMP.

Notes
- Operational risk: removing the exclusion allows all addresses in the /24 to be leased; consider creating a static DHCP binding for .12 if you need a guaranteed address.

Verification

Notes
